refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall provide analysis of the worksite terrain.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR includes multiple obligations: analysis of terrain, obstacles, and work-zone representation. To be atomic and solution-neutral, these should be separated into distinct FRs focusing on one aspect each. This refined FR focuses solely on terrain analysis, avoiding any implementation details or combined requirements.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify that the system can generate and present an analysis of the terrain features of the worksite independently of other site features.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall provide analysis of the worksite terrain.
The NGHE solution shall provide analysis of obstacles within the worksite area.
The NGHE solution shall provide representation of the work-zone within the worksite area.

candidate_dps: Generate a digital terrain model representing key site features
Extract and classify terrain features relevant to safety and operations
Create a geospatial map integrating terrain data with safety and policy overlays
Provide an interface for planning and execution modules to query terrain information
selected_dp.selected_dp: Generate a digital terrain model representing key site features

potential_sources: Integration of terrain data with safety and operational policies may introduce coupling if not modularized
coupling_check.notes: Selecting a DP focused on generating a terrain model keeps the solution modular and allows separate handling of safety and policy overlays in downstream processes.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability of accurate raw terrain data for modeling
Risk that terrain model may not capture all features required by safety and operational policies
Assumes planning/execution functions can consume the terrain model format provided
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly separating terrain modeling from safety and policy integration to maintain modularity and reduce coupling in the overall system design.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the processing and generation of digital terrain models from site data, including key site feature extraction and representation.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives raw sensor and site data necessary for terrain model generation.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives commands or requests to generate or update digital terrain models.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Sends generated digital terrain models and related data to other system components or external users.

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as terrain model generation is a core processing function of NGHE Solution; minimal diffusion as data inputs and outputs are well encapsulated.
Vehicle module primarily handles mobility and control; terrain model generation would require significant diffusion outside this module.
Teleoperation focuses on remote control and monitoring; including terrain model generation would cause high diffusion and low cohesion.
Safety Services is focused on alerts and safety monitoring; terrain model generation is unrelated and would cause poor containment.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary for containing the digital terrain model generation DP because it naturally aligns with processing site data and generating environmental models, minimizing diffusion and maximizing cohesion.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on timely and accurate telemetry data input which may affect model accuracy.
Interface changes in Telemetry or Network Link could impact module integration.
If terrain model generation requires real-time updates, performance constraints may challenge module boundaries.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP 'Generate a digital terrain model representing key site features' directly satisfies the FR 'The NGHE solution shall provide analysis of the worksite terrain' by producing a digital representation of the terrain, which is a clear and measurable functional decomposition aligned with the acceptance criteria of generating a worksite model/map available to planning and execution functions.

dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The DP is well contained within the NGHE Solution module, which has high cohesion (containment score 8) and low diffusion (diffusion score 2). The module boundaries and interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, Network Link) are well defined and encapsulate the terrain model generation functionality, minimizing diffusion risk. Other modules show poor containment and high diffusion scores for this DP, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the implementation module.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through the availability of the generated digital terrain model to planning and execution functions, as per acceptance criteria. The external interfaces provide clear data flow paths for input (Telemetry, Tasking) and output (Network Link), enabling traceability and testing.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The ownership candidate 'NGHE Solution' clearly aligns with the FR and DP, with well-defined scope and interfaces, ensuring clarity in responsibility and module boundaries.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with strong justification, containment, and verification feasibility. The DP fully satisfies the FR with clear measurable criteria, the DP is well contained within a high-cohesion, low-diffusion implementation module, and verification is feasible with clear ownership and interfaces. There are no recommended revisions and no diffusion risks. Given the high safety criticality, it is prudent to stop decomposition here to maintain clarity and control rather than adding complexity unnecessarily.

assumptions: The DP 'Generate a digital terrain model representing key site features' fully captures the FR 'The NGHE solution shall provide analysis of the worksite terrain'.
The NGHE Solution module is the appropriate implementation boundary with high cohesion and low diffusion scores.
Verification methods and interfaces are sufficient to confirm functional and implementation correctness.
evidence_needed: Verification results confirming the digital terrain model meets acceptance criteria.
Module interface documentation confirming containment and low diffusion.
